frameworks.name: hybrid neural-cognitive model
frameworks.one_line: RL algorithms combined with artificial neural networks to fit choice behavior

frameworks.name: incremental reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: standard account assuming choices depend on incrementally updated summary variables

frameworks.name: flexible memory system
frameworks.one_line: memory that encodes rich past-event representations over multiple timescales

core_question: Can human reward-guided learning be explained by purely incremental reinforcement learning, or does it require richer memory representations?
key_assumptions: neural-network components can capture residual structure beyond RL summary variables
behavior on probabilistic reward tasks indexes underlying learning mechanisms
manipulations: probabilistic reward-learning task in a large human dataset
model class varied between pure incremental RL and hybrid RL+ANN
key_findings: no algorithm based solely on incremental updating of choice variables explains the behavior
hybrid models incorporating neural networks better capture human choices
behavior is consistent with memory representations spanning multiple timescales
interpretations: human reward learning depends on a flexible memory system encoding rich event representations
reward-guided choices rely on more elaborate memory than previously believed
